verdict_reasoning: EREG encodes a 162-aa type I transmembrane proligand. The TM precursor traffics through the secretory pathway to the cell surface (basolateral in polarized epithelial cells), where it supports juxtacrine signaling. However, ADAM17 constitutively cleaves it within 1–2 hours to release the 46-aa mature peptide, making the soluble shed form the dominant biological actor. The TM-anchored stage is real but short-lived; the surface pool is continuously depleted by shedding. This matches the transient-TM-proligand pattern: a contextual surface presence with the shed soluble form as the canonical signaling entity.
